title: Control of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s Uranium Stockpiles Were Sticking Points
summary: The U.S. had demanded that Iran immediately reopen the strait to all maritime traffic, but Iran said it would do so only after a final peace deal, according to Iranian officials.

title: What Now? Vance Leaves Iran Talks Without a Deal.
summary: The lack of a breakthrough after 21 hours of negotiations leaves the Trump administration facing several unpalatable options.

title: In Pakistan Talks, Iran Saw a U.S. Trying to Dictate, Not Negotiate
summary: Iran sees American demands as reaching far beyond what it achieved in war. Tehran is gambling that it can withstand further bombardment more than Washington is willing to sustain economic chaos, experts say.

title: Manhattan DA Opens Investigation Into Eric Swalwell After Sexual Assault Allegations
summary: The Manhattan district attorney’s office is examining a claim that Representative Eric Swalwell, a candidate for governor in California, assaulted a woman in New York City in 2024.

title: Push to Expel Swalwell Could Touch Off Chain Reaction of House Removal Votes
summary: The House could move within days to consider expelling Representative Eric Swalwell, Democrat of California, in light of sexual assault allegations against him. There could be more to come for both parties.

title: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations
summary: Accusations that Mr. Swalwell, a congressman running for governor in California, sexually assaulted a former staffer and behaved inappropriately with other women have upended the race.

title: Judges Fired After Blocking Deportations of Pro-Palestinian Students
summary: The immigration judges’ abrupt dismissals marked the latest efforts by the Trump administration to reshape the country’s immigration courts.

title: Second Venezuelan Doctor Detained in South Texas by Immigration Agents
summary: An E.R. doctor was detained Saturday, just days after a family physician had been detained. Both were traveling when immigration agents took them into custody.

title: Postal Service, Under Pressure, Now Faces Trump’s Mail Ballot Order
summary: President Trump’s executive order, which has been challenged as unconstitutional, would limit the Postal Service to sending only the ballots of voters deemed eligible.

title: Many Polls Say Orban Will Lose. But He Has an Edge Even Before Voting Begins.
summary: Over 16 years, Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party has repeatedly tweaked the electoral system to its advantage, making the vote free, but not entirely fair.

title: What to Know as Hungary Votes in Elections Watched by the World
summary: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a standard-bearer for global populism, in political peril, the vote on Sunday could have far-reaching implications.

title: The Escalating Global A.I. Arms Race
summary: China, the U.S., Russia and others have ramped up their contest over artificial-intelligence-backed weapons and military systems. The buildup has been compared to the dawn of the nuclear weapons age.

title: Someone Has to Be Happy. Why Not Lauren Sánchez Bezos?
summary: As half of an unfathomably powerful couple, Mrs. Sánchez Bezos seems to have influenced the uber-rich to stop apologizing, and start enjoying themselves.

title: Trump’s War With Iran Has Weakened America
summary: The president should at long last recognize the ineptitude of his impulsive, go-it-alone approach.

title: Hasan Piker Is Not the Enemy
summary: Conversation is not a reward to be bestowed on those with whom we agree; it’s a base line practice.

title: What Do Vegetative Patients Know?
summary: They may have more awareness than we thought.

title: Mass Stampede at Haitian Tourist Site Leaves Dozens Dead
summary: The tragedy took place at the Citadelle Laferrière, a historic fortress in northern Haiti and one of the country’s defining symbols.

title: What the ‘Real Housewives’ Think About Congress’s Reality TV Drama
summary: A visit to the Capitol by stars of the “Real Housewives” offered a glimpse into how the nation’s elected representatives mimic the gossipy world of reality TV. Nobody appears to relish the similarities.

title: Philly Teens Are Trying to Make Safer Neighborhoods Through Music
summary: Teens in Philadelphia are trying to make safer neighborhoods. Many are starting with music.

title: Shannan Gilbert’s Death Remains the Great Unsolved Mystery of the Gilgo Beach Murders
summary: The death of 23-year-old Shannan Gilbert remains the great unsolved mystery surrounding the Gilgo Beach serial killer case.
